Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 5, 259 [Šumma Alu 21a commentary][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
o 1'o 1'

[...] ina ITI ša₂ ana da-li

(o 1') [...] during the month which, to roam around

o 2'2'

[...]-ma : u₂-šam-ṭe-e-ma

(o 2') [...] and : and he will suffer reduction.

o 3'3'

x [...] ši-te-ʾu-[u₂] [...] x ⸢U₂⸣ : ina KISLAḪ₃ EN.TE.NA : dan-na-tu₂ : ku-ṣu

(o 3') ... [...] to look all over [...] ...; in the depth of winter : (KISLAH means) depth : (EN.TE.NA means) winter.

o 4'4'

ek-let nam-rat : [ana muš]-ke-ni qa-bi : ša₂-lum-mat : bal-tu₄ : du-u₂-tu₄ : bal-tu₄

(o 4') She is dim (is the opposite) of she is bright. : It is said [about] the commoner. : Radiance (is a synonym of) : dignity; : virility (is a synonym of): dignity; virility (is a synonym of): good looks. : You will fill a namhāru-vessel and: you will set (it) up correctly like an agubbû-vessel.

o 5'5'

du-u₂-tu₄ : bu-nu : nam-ḫa-ra SA₅-ma : ki-ma dugA.GUB₂.BA tu-ka-ni-ma

o 6'6'

⸢ša₂⸣ MUŠ GAZ-ma TI-ma ana ŠA₃ KI KU₄ : ša₂ MUŠ i-du-ku-ma iš-šu-u₂ iq-bi-ri : TI : la-qu-u

(o 6') He who kills a snake and takes (it) and puts it into the earth (means): he who kills a snake and lifts (it and) buries (it); : TI (means): to take.

o 7'7'

ša₂ KU₆ [i]-duk-ma ana ŠA₃ KI KU₄ : TI : ba-la-ṭu : NIG₂.ŠU-šu DU₃-šu₂ KI.MIN TUR-šu₂ GU₇ : NIG₂.ŠU-šu DU₃-šu₂

(o 7') He who kills a fish and puts (it) into the earth. : TI (also means): to live. : He will give up his goods for him; alternatively, he will enjoy his goods. : He will give up his goods for him DU₃-šu (meaning) he will give up for him (other writing): i-zib-šu₂; : DU₃ (means): to give up; : TUR-šu₂ GU₇ (means): he will enjoy his goods; : TUR (means): goods1.

o 8'8'

iz-zib₂-šu₂ : i-zib-šu₂ : DU₃ : e-ze-bu : TUR-šu₂ GU₇ : bu-šu-šu₂ ik-kal : TUR : bu-šu-u₂

o 9'9'

x : ṣe-ri₃ : lem-nu : uš-ta-aḫ-ma : u₂-ši-iḫ-ma : ⸢UR₅⸣ta šu-a-tu₂ : ina qe₂-reb ⸢GABA⸣-[šu₂]

(o 9') ... : Snake (is a synonym of) evil one. : ... : .... : UR₅-ta (means) his. : it lies twined across his chest and moves towards his skull (the whole sentence means): it moves around (meaning): it drags. : It keeps eating (written): DU.DU (meaning): to eat each other2; RA₂.RA₂ (means): to beat each other. : (If) a snake falls onto a man's shoulder: the man's supporter will die. : Shoulder (is a synonym of) back; GU₂.TAR (means): the man's back (which means): rear. d-MUŠ (means): nirāhu-snake (that is): a narrow snake which is very long.

o 10'10'

⸢i?⸣-tag-gar-ma ana muḫ-ḫi-šu₂ i-ku-šu₂ : im-ṣur : im-šur : i-tak-kal : DU.DU : it-ku-lu

o 11'11'

RA₂.RA₂ : mit-ḫu-ṣu : * MUŠ ana bu-di NA ŠUBut mu-kil ku₂-tal₂-li NA UŠ₂ : bu-di : ku-tal-la

o 12'12'

GU₂.TAR : ku-tal₂-li NA : ku-tal-li dMUŠ : ni-ra-ḫu : MUŠ qat₂-nu ša₂ ma-gal ar₂-ki

o 13'13'

21 * IRI ina SUKUD GARin la₃ AL.TIL

(o 13') (Tablet) 21 (of the series): "(If) a city is located on a height". Not completed.

o 14'14'

* [x x] UGU NA ša₂ KI na-gi-ru-u₂-<<ru>>-tu₂ di-in-šu₂ GID₂.DA GAL₂ : iṣ-bur-ma : is-si-ma

(o 14') [(If) ...] on top of a man whose (case has been proclaimed in) the district (by) the office of herald: there will be a long trial for him.3 : It hisses (means): he proclaims.

o 15'15'

[MUŠEN] ZI.ZI : ṣa-bar ša₂ iṣ-ṣur : MUŠEN ZI.ZI : ši-si-tu₂ : u₄-mi ri-qi₂ : U₄ 29-KAM

(o 15') MUŠEN ZI.ZI (means): the twittering of a bird, (written): MUŠEN. ZI.ZI (means): cry. : Moonless day (means): day 29 (of the month).

o 16'16'

[...] u₂-šu-uz-zu : * MUŠ NA IGI-ma GAZ-šu₂ NA BI a-di-ra-tu-šu₂ NU TE-MEŠ-šu₂

(o 16') [...] to triumph. : (If) a man sees a snake and kills it: as for that man, misfortunes will not approach him.

o 17'17'

[...] x x-ma a-na da-a-ku ṭa-a-bu IGI-ma i-dak : aš-šum U₄ 20-KAM ša₂ itiGU₄

(o 17') [...] ... he shall observe that it is favourable for killing and he shall kill: concerning day 20 of Ayyaru (Month II).

Reverse
r 1r 1

i-ta-dir : ip-ta-ṭir : ša₂ [...] x [...] u₂-ka-si-ma ⸢NAM?⸣

(r 1) It has become afraid (means): it has broken loose. : he who [...] ... [...] binds and ...

r 22

x ina? KI? KUR? x x [...] DU₃.A.BI : [...] ma-la dUTU nap-ḫi

(r 2) ... in ... [...] all of it: [...] as much as the sun is bright

r 33

[x] TA [...] ḫa-ba-bu : ḫa-ṣa-nu : ḫa-ba-bu : na-ša₂-qa

(r 3) [...] ... [...] to caress (is a synonym of): to hug; to caress (is also a synonym of): to kiss

r 44

[... i]-⸢ram⸣-mu-um : ri-gim ša₂-kin-ma

(r 4) [...] he will roar (means): the voice is raised.

r 55

[...] ⸢sar⸣-ra-ti šak-na-ti

(r 5) [...] queens (is a synonym of) female officials.

r 66

[... ḫa-di]-nu : le-mut-tu₂

(r 6) [...] a malevolent rejoicer (is a synonym of): evil.

r 77

[...]-⸢ma⸣ DU.DUak

(r 7) [...] and he will roam around.

(rest of reverse missing)

1 The equivalences DU₃ = ezēbu and TUR = būšu are not attested elsewhere.

2 There is a probable scribal confusion between akālu and alāku ; the latter is expected here.

3 This line is probably corrupt.
